meta.title: RCB vs GT · Final
meta.subtitle: IPL · Ahmedabad Stadium · 7:30 PM
meta.disclaimer: Generated before match start · May not reflect live events

blocks.content: GT plays at home where they know every inch of the pitch. However, RCB is fearless here, having lifted the trophy at this exact venue last year. Both teams are mentally peaked.
This outfield is massive, making boundary-hitting difficult. The team that sprints hard between the wickets and limits dot balls will have a massive edge over pure power hitters.
Over 100,000 local fans will be roaring for GT, creating an intense, high-pressure cauldron for the visitors.
Impact Players are deciding matches this season. They sub in during the most critical moments to completely break the opponent's rhythm.
Mohit (GT) is a master at the death overs, using slow balls to force bad shots. Patidar (RCB) comes out swinging if early wickets fall, instantly shifting momentum back to his team.
If Kohli can safely navigate Rashid's tricky spin without getting out, RCB will easily control the middle of the game.
Glenn Maxwell (RCB) is dealing with back pain. His availability for the final is currently doubtful.
